company.name: Japan Trip Companion
company.goal: Build the ultimate Japan trip companion web app for a 2-week cherry blossom trip (Tokyo, Kyoto, Osaka, Nara). The existing site is at ~/japan-trip/ with an interactive Leaflet map and day-by-day sidebar. Enhance it into a world-class travel guide with: (1) Interactive maps with walking/transit route directions between stops, estimated travel times, and Google Maps deep links. (2) Trilingual support — toggle between English, Chinese (Simplified), and Japanese for place names, descriptions, and key phrases. (3) A 'What's Next' mode that highlights the current/next activity based on time of day. (4) Useful Japanese travel phrases with pronunciation for each context (restaurant, train, temple). (5) Restaurant reservation details, confirmation numbers, and direct links. (6) Cherry blossom forecast integration. (7) Offline-friendly PWA with service worker so it works without internet in Japan. (8) Weather widget showing forecast for each day. (9) A packing/checklist feature with local storage persistence. (10) Mobile-first responsive design optimized for using on-the-go.

tasks.title: Implement Google Directions API integration with Leaflet polyline rendering
tasks.description:
FILES TO CREATE: config.js (API keys), routes.js (routing logic). FILES TO MODIFY: script.js (map rendering), index.html (CDN script tags), styles.css (route styling). DEPENDENCIES: (1) Google Maps JavaScript API key from https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials - enable Directions API. (2) Polyline decoding: Use @mapbox/polyline package via CDN OR implement manual decoder. IMPLEMENTATION: (1) config.js exports const CONFIG = {GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY: '', MAPS_DEEP_LINK_BASE: 'https://www.google.com/maps/dir/?api=1'}. (2) routes.js exports async fetchRoute(origin, destination, mode='transit') → fetch(`https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/directions/json?origin=${origin.lat},${origin.lng}&destination=${destination.lat},${destination.lng}&mode=${mode}&key=${CONFIG.GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY}`), parse response.routes[0].legs[0] for {duration: leg.duration.text, distance: leg.distance.text, polyline: leg.overview_polyline.points}. (3) script.js adds drawRoutes(dayItinerary) → for each consecutive stop pair, await fetchRoute(), decode polyline with polyline.decode(), create L.polyline(coords, {color: '#4285F4', weight: 3, opacity: 0.7}).addTo(map). (4) Add L.divIcon midpoint markers with innerHTML: `
${duration}
`. (5) Modify marker popup template to append: `📍 Directions`. (6) Update itinerary.json schema to include routes array per day. ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (a) Blue polylines render between all consecutive stops on map. (b) Travel time badges display at polyline midpoints. (c) Clicking 'Directions' link opens Google Maps with destination pre-filled. (d) API errors show fallback message 'Route unavailable'. (e) Console logs show no CORS or API key errors.

tasks.title: Integrate OpenWeatherMap API with 6-hour localStorage caching
tasks.description:
FILES TO CREATE: weather.js (API logic). FILES TO MODIFY: config.js (API key), script.js (widget rendering), index.html (weather containers), styles.css (card styling). DEPENDENCIES: OpenWeatherMap API key - sign up at https://openweathermap.org/api, free tier allows 1000 calls/day. IMPLEMENTATION: (1) config.js add OPENWEATHER_API_KEY: ''. (2) weather.js exports async fetchWeather(city, lat, lon) → check cache: const cached = localStorage.getItem(`weather_${city}`); if (cached) { const {data, timestamp} = JSON.parse(cached); if (Date.now() - timestamp < 6 * 60 * 60 * 1000) return data; }. (3) Fetch: const res = await fetch(`https://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/forecast/daily?lat=${lat}&lon=${lon}&cnt=14&units=metric&appid=${CONFIG.OPENWEATHER_API_KEY}`); const json = await res.json(); const weatherData = json.list.map(day => ({date: dayjs(day.dt * 1000).format('YYYY-MM-DD'), icon: day.weather[0].icon, tempHigh: Math.round(day.temp.max), tempLow: Math.round(day.temp.min), description: day.weather[0].description})). (4) Cache: localStorage.setItem(`weather_${city}`, JSON.stringify({data: weatherData, timestamp: Date.now()})). (5) index.html add
to each day section. (6) script.js add renderWeather(city, date, containerId) → const weather = await fetchWeather(CITIES[city].lat, CITIES[city].lon); const dayWeather = weather.find(w => w.date === date); document.getElementById(containerId).innerHTML = `
${dayWeather.tempHigh}°/${dayWeather.tempLow}°

${dayWeather.description}

`. (7) CITIES constant: const CITIES = {Tokyo: {lat: 35.6762, lon: 139.6503}, Kyoto: {lat: 35.0116, lon: 135.7681}, Osaka: {lat: 34.6937, lon: 135.5023}, Nara: {lat: 34.6851, lon: 135.8048}}. (8) Add refresh button: . ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (a) Weather displays for each day with icon, high/low temps, description. (b) Data cached in localStorage for 6 hours. (c) Refresh button clears cache and refetches. (d) Loading state shows 'Loading weather...' during fetch. (e) Error state shows 'Weather unavailable' on API failure. (f) No duplicate API calls on same day within cache window.

tasks.title: Implement PWA Infrastructure with Offline-First Service Worker
tasks.description:
**Files to Create:**
- `/manifest.json` — PWA manifest with name 'Japan Trip 2026', short_name 'Japan Trip', start_url '/', display 'standalone', theme_color '#ef4444', background_color '#0f0f14'. Icons at 192x192 and 512x512 (generate from existing design or use placeholder).
- `/service-worker.js` — Service worker with 3 caches: (1) 'static-v1' for HTML/CSS/JS/fonts, (2) 'leaflet-v1' for Leaflet library and CSS, (3) 'tiles-v1' for map tiles. Use Cache-First strategy for static assets. For map tiles, implement Network-First with 7-day cache fallback. Pre-cache critical tiles for Tokyo (35.65-35.75, 139.65-139.85), Kyoto (34.95-35.05, 135.70-135.80), Osaka (34.63-34.73, 135.45-135.55), Nara (34.66-34.72, 135.80-135.88) at zoom levels 12-16.
- `/offline.html` — Fallback page with inline CSS (no external deps), message 'Offline Mode - Map tiles may be limited', and link back to index.
- `/icons/icon-192.png`, `/icons/icon-512.png` — App icons (can use simple placeholder with 'Japan' text for now).

**Files to Modify:**
- `index.html` — Add `<link rel="manifest" href="/manifest.json">`, add `<meta name="theme-color" content="#ef4444">`, register service worker with: `if('serviceWorker' in navigator){navigator.serviceWorker.register('/service-worker.js')}`
- `script.js` — Add install prompt handler: listen for 'beforeinstallprompt' event, store event, show custom install button in UI (add to header). Add offline indicator in header when `!navigator.onLine`.

**Key Implementation Details:**
- Use Workbox library (CDN: `importScripts('https://storage.googleapis.com/workbox-cdn/releases/6.5.4/workbox-sw.js')`) for simpler cache management OR implement vanilla Service Worker API with fetch interceptor.
- Tile pre-caching: In service worker install event, generate tile URLs using pattern `https://{s}.basemaps.cartocdn.com/dark_all/{z}/{x}/{y}{r}.png` for specified lat/lng bounds and zoom levels. Calculate tile coordinates using: `tileX = floor((lng + 180) / 360 * 2^zoom)`, `tileY = floor((1 - ln(tan(lat*π/180) + 1/cos(lat*π/180)) / π) / 2 * 2^zoom)`.
- Static cache list: ['/', '/index.html', '/style.css', '/script.js', '/data.js', 'https://unpkg.com/leaflet@1.9.4/dist/leaflet.css', 'https://unpkg.com/leaflet@1.9.4/dist/leaflet.js', 'https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@300;400;500;600;700&family=Noto+Sans+JP:wght@300;400;500;700&display=swap'].

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Lighthouse PWA score >90.
- App installs on mobile (iOS Safari Add to Home Screen, Android Chrome Install App).
- App loads offline with cached content.
- Map tiles for main cities (Tokyo/Kyoto/Osaka/Nara) available offline at zoom 12-16.
- Offline indicator appears when network unavailable.
- Install prompt appears once and can be dismissed.

childTasks.title: Implement PWA Infrastructure with Offline-First Service Worker
childTasks.description:
**Files to Create:**
- `/manifest.json` — PWA manifest with name 'Japan Trip 2026', short_name 'Japan Trip', start_url '/', display 'standalone', theme_color '#ef4444', background_color '#0f0f14'. Icons at 192x192 and 512x512 (generate from existing design or use placeholder).
- `/service-worker.js` — Service worker with 3 caches: (1) 'static-v1' for HTML/CSS/JS/fonts, (2) 'leaflet-v1' for Leaflet library and CSS, (3) 'tiles-v1' for map tiles. Use Cache-First strategy for static assets. For map tiles, implement Network-First with 7-day cache fallback. Pre-cache critical tiles for Tokyo (35.65-35.75, 139.65-139.85), Kyoto (34.95-35.05, 135.70-135.80), Osaka (34.63-34.73, 135.45-135.55), Nara (34.66-34.72, 135.80-135.88) at zoom levels 12-16.
- `/offline.html` — Fallback page with inline CSS (no external deps), message 'Offline Mode - Map tiles may be limited', and link back to index.
- `/icons/icon-192.png`, `/icons/icon-512.png` — App icons (can use simple placeholder with 'Japan' text for now).

**Files to Modify:**
- `index.html` — Add `<link rel="manifest" href="/manifest.json">`, add `<meta name="theme-color" content="#ef4444">`, register service worker with: `if('serviceWorker' in navigator){navigator.serviceWorker.register('/service-worker.js')}`
- `script.js` — Add install prompt handler: listen for 'beforeinstallprompt' event, store event, show custom install button in UI (add to header). Add offline indicator in header when `!navigator.onLine`.

**Key Implementation Details:**
- Use Workbox library (CDN: `importScripts('https://storage.googleapis.com/workbox-cdn/releases/6.5.4/workbox-sw.js')`) for simpler cache management OR implement vanilla Service Worker API with fetch interceptor.
- Tile pre-caching: In service worker install event, generate tile URLs using pattern `https://{s}.basemaps.cartocdn.com/dark_all/{z}/{x}/{y}{r}.png` for specified lat/lng bounds and zoom levels. Calculate tile coordinates using: `tileX = floor((lng + 180) / 360 * 2^zoom)`, `tileY = floor((1 - ln(tan(lat*π/180) + 1/cos(lat*π/180)) / π) / 2 * 2^zoom)`.
- Static cache list: ['/', '/index.html', '/style.css', '/script.js', '/data.js', 'https://unpkg.com/leaflet@1.9.4/dist/leaflet.css', 'https://unpkg.com/leaflet@1.9.4/dist/leaflet.js', 'https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@300;400;500;600;700&family=Noto+Sans+JP:wght@300;400;500;700&display=swap'].

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Lighthouse PWA score >90.
- App installs on mobile (iOS Safari Add to Home Screen, Android Chrome Install App).
- App loads offline with cached content.
- Map tiles for main cities (Tokyo/Kyoto/Osaka/Nara) available offline at zoom 12-16.
- Offline indicator appears when network unavailable.
- Install prompt appears once and can be dismissed.
